title: Central bank gold statistics: Central banks remain committed to gold
gist: Central banks bought a net 41t of gold in May 2026, led by Poland (18t) and China (10t), while a record-high 45% of surveyed central bankers expect to grow their own gold reserves over the next 12 months. New demand is emerging in Latin America and the Bank of Korea is preparing to access gold via ETFs.

themes.theme: May 2026 net flows and buyer concentration

insights.claim: Official global gold reserves rose by a net 41 tonnes in May 2026, with purchases concentrated among a small, familiar group of buyers rather than broadening out.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Steady official demand at ~40t/month is a structural price support, but concentration means the trend is vulnerable to a handful of buyers pausing.
insights.quote: official gold reserves increased by a net 41t during the month, with purchases once again concentrated among a familiar cast of buyers
insights.locator: Central banks were back in buying mode in May

insights.claim: May 2026 buying was driven by Poland (18t) and China (10t), with Uzbekistan and Kazakhstan continuing monthly net accumulation.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: EM central banks remain the marginal buyer of gold; watch these four names for month-over-month continuation as the demand tell.
insights.locator: Much of the activity was driven by Poland

insights.claim: Singapore's Monetary Authority bought a net 4t of gold in May 2026, its first monthly net purchase since September 2025, lifting total holdings to 197t.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A returning buyer widens the demand base; Singapore re-entering after an eight-month pause signals renewed institutional appetite.
insights.locator: The Monetary Authority of Singapore purchased 4t

insights.claim: The Monetary Authority of Singapore plans to launch central bank gold-vaulting services in October 2026 as part of building a gold hub in the city-state.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: New vaulting/clearing infrastructure in Asia could shift physical gold trade flows and settlement away from London/Zurich over time.
insights.quote: the MAS is looking to establish central bank gold vaulting services October 2026, in line with the country's plans to establish a gold hub in the city state.
insights.locator: Separately, the MAS is looking to establish central bank gold vaulting services

insights.claim: Poland's central bank added 18t in May 2026 — its fourth consecutive month of double-digit net buying — bringing holdings to 614t as it moves toward a stated 700t target.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: An explicit 700t target with 86t still to buy gives a quantifiable runway of forward Polish demand to track.
insights.quote: Poland now holds 614t of gold in its reserves, inching closer to its 700t target
insights.locator: The National Bank of Poland continued its net buying trajectory

insights.claim: Poland has accumulated 64t of gold year-to-date in 2026, leading all central banks, ahead of Uzbekistan (33t) and China (25t).
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Poland is the single largest official gold buyer of 2026 so far, making it the most important name for demand forecasts.

insights.claim: The People's Bank of China added 10t in May 2026, its 20th consecutive month of net buying and its largest monthly addition since December 2024, leaving official reserves around 2,331t or 9% of total reserves.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: China at only 9% gold-to-reserves versus EM peers implies large latent buying capacity if diversification continues; a 20-month streak signals policy persistence.
insights.quote: China's official gold reserves now stand at 9% of total reserves or around 2,331t
insights.locator: In its 20th consecutive month of net buying

insights.claim: Uzbekistan holds 87% of its total reserves in gold and Kazakhstan 78%, versus China's 9%, showing wide dispersion in how EM central banks weight gold.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: The gap between high-allocation (Uzbekistan, Kazakhstan) and low-allocation (China) banks maps where future diversification demand can still come from.
insights.locator: The Central Bank of Uzbekistan accumulated 9t

insights.claim: The Czech National Bank bought 2t in May 2026, extending a consistent run of net gold purchases to 39 straight months.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A 39-month advanced-economy buying streak shows the diversification trend is not confined to EMs or sanctioned states.
insights.locator: The Czech National Bank and the Central Bank of Jordan also bought gold

themes.theme: Central Bank Gold Reserves Survey 2026 sentiment

insights.claim: In the World Gold Council's ninth Central Bank Gold Reserves Survey (2026), 89% of central bankers expect global gold reserves to rise over the next 12 months.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Near-unanimous expectation of rising official reserves is a forward demand signal underpinning the bull case for gold.
insights.quote: 89% of central bankers expect global gold reserves to increase in the next 12 months
insights.locator: As published in our ninth Central Bank Gold Reserves Survey 2026

insights.claim: A record-high 45% of central bankers expect their own institution's gold reserves to increase over the next 12 months.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: The intent-to-buy metric hitting a survey record is a leading indicator that official demand should stay firm into 2027.
insights.quote: a record high 45% of central bankers expect their own institution's gold reserves to increase over the next 12 months
insights.locator: Meanwhile, a record high 45% of central bankers

insights.claim: Only 4% of surveyed central banks purchase gold via gold-backed ETFs, with OTC markets remaining the dominant accumulation channel.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: ETF adoption by central banks is a nascent channel; any broadening beyond 4% would add a new, price-visible demand stream for gold ETFs.
insights.quote: only 4% of survey respondents indicated that they purchase gold via gold-backed ETFs
insights.locator: Gaining exposure to gold via ETFs is fairly uncommon amongst central banks

themes.theme: Bank of Korea ETF access and diversification

insights.claim: The Bank of Korea has reportedly completed preparations to invest in overseas gold-backed ETFs as part of foreign currency asset diversification, though it has not confirmed whether any allocation was made.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A large advanced-economy bank entering via ETFs would validate a new demand channel and could catalyze similar moves by peers.
insights.quote: The Bank of Korea has reportedly completed preparations to invest in overseas gold-backed ETFs as part of its strategy of foreign currency asset diversification
insights.locator: The Bank of Korea readies allocation to gold ETF

insights.claim: The Bank of Korea holds 104t of gold, about 3% of total reserves — low versus emerging-market peers — after having stopped buying gold for 13 years.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A 3% allocation after a 13-year pause implies significant room to add; a resumption of Korean buying would be a fresh incremental demand source.
insights.quote: The bank currently holds 104t of gold in its reserves – about 3% of its total reserves – a figure that is relatively low compared to its emerging market peers.
insights.locator: The Bank of Korea readies allocation to gold ETF

insights.claim: Gold-backed ETFs were the Bank of Korea's chosen instrument because of their high liquidity and lower storage costs.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: If liquidity and storage-cost advantages drive more official buyers into ETFs, it redirects part of central bank demand into listed vehicles rather than physical OTC.
insights.locator: According to the report, gold-backed ETFs were the instrument of choice

themes.theme: Latin American central banks as an emerging buyer bloc

insights.claim: New gold buying appeared among Latin American central banks in 2026, with Chile accumulating around 8t year-to-date, followed by Guatemala (2t), and Bolivia and Uruguay at 1t each.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A new regional bloc entering is an early, second-order demand signal; watch whether it broadens to larger Latin American peers.
insights.locator: Gold gains traction within Latin American central banks

insights.claim: It is too early to tell whether emerging Latin American gold buying will gain traction or broaden to match other regional peers, despite recent geopolitical tension in the region.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: The author flags this as unconfirmed, so treat Latin American demand as a watch-item rather than a base-case driver.
insights.quote: it is too early to tell if this trend will gain traction or broaden out to match other Latin American peers
insights.locator: While this region has seen some geopolitical heat recently

insights.claim: Russia's central bank sold 6t of gold in May 2026 and 34t year-to-date, lowering total holdings to 2,292t.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A persistent seller partially offsets EM buying; sustained Russian selling could cap the pace of net official accumulation.
insights.locator: The Central Bank of Russia continued its streak of net selling

insights.claim: Turkey's central bank sold 3t in May 2026, bringing year-to-date sales to 81t — the largest official net selling of the year.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
entities: Central Bank of the Republic of Turkey
insights.so_what: Turkey's 81t of sales is the biggest single drag on net demand; its selling is often domestic FX-management driven, so watch lira dynamics.
insights.locator: The Central Bank of the Republic of Turkey sold 3t
